name: The Poetics of Time: A Poetic Immersion into Visual Arts
description: Dillipoetry, in collaboration with Anant Art Gallery, invites you to The Poetics of Time, a one-of-a-kind interactive poetry workshop where poetry and visual art​ coalesce into a shared experience. As one artist observed, “art is not a history of grand​ masterpieces; it is a record of human attempts.” This holds equally true for poetry, the​ experience is yet another attempt to bring the two into a​n embodied dialogue.The workshop anchors itself in the themes of reverie, pause, and rupture—how​ they unfold in​ one’s inner life and in the world around us, personally,​ systemically and politically.​ Conceived as a response to the ongoing exhibition​ Reverie. Pause. Rupture, the workshop​ engages with the exhibition through​ poetry. The group show, featuring leading contemporary​ artists, “challenges the​ artist and viewer alike to express what may be inexpressible. It reflects​ on time,​ its apparent continuity, as well as the sudden breaks that give rise to new​ beginnings,”​ notes curator Gayatri Sinha.The two-hour-long workshop with its reflective prompts and guided exercises​ promises to bring​ an absurd interplay of visual, somatic and creative mediums to help us move through the idea of​ personal imagination — with prescribed​ writings written across a wide timeline by some of the​ wise voices like Gaston Bachelard, Audre Lorde, Mary Oliver, Naomi Shihab Nye among others.About the host: Rachit is a poet and founder of Dillipoetry—a literary platform known for its thematic, experimental poetry walks, workshops, and training programmes. He is the former Creative Head at Youth Alliance, where he designed and facilitated transformational leadership journeys for young social leaders across India. With over a decade in the social development sector, his work spans diverse social issues. His poetry has appeared in 25+ literary platforms and anthologies. His recent series Memories of Yami and The Asthma City—tracing the Yamuna River through Delhi and the city’s escalating air crisis—were published by Down To Earth magazine.Rachit has been selected for international residencies, including Language is a Queer Thing, a programme by The Queer Muslim Project in collaboration with the British Council, UK. He received the Germinator Grant by Ecoversity for Dillipoetry’s regenerative approach to arts and community.He has performed twice at BBC Contains Strong Language—the UK’s largest poetry festival (Leeds 2023, Bradford 2025)—and at institutions including the French Institute India, Goethe-Institut, British Council, and Alliance Française. His work has appeared on BBC Radio 3’s The Verb. He also served as Lead Poet and Advisor to O.P. Jindal Global University’s programme, We Are Meant to Sing as One.

location.address: B-5/3, Block B 5, Safdarjung Enclave, New Delhi, Delhi 110029, India

name: Identity in Transition: A Psycho-Social Analysis
description: Join us for a lecture that dives into a transformation happening in our pockets and on our screens, one that is reshaping who we are and how we feel. About the LectureOur identity used to be shaped by our face-to-face relationships and physical communities. Today, much of that construction has moved online. But what happens to our mental health when our "self" is curated for an audience?This lecture explores the dynamic relationship between digital identity and psychological well-being. Moving beyond simple critiques of screen time, it examines the core dimensions of the self - personal, social, and collective, and how they shift in a digital landscape.The session explores:The Architecture of the Digital Self. How social media allows for identity experimentation and curated self-presentation.The psychological mechanics of social comparison, FOMO, and the quest for external validation through likes and comments.Understanding the tension and anxiety that arise from discrepancies between our online and offline selves.How these platforms offer vital spaces for social support, belonging, and mental health awareness, the digital silver lining.As we navigate an increasingly hybrid world, this session invites us to consider how digital literacy and mindful engagement can help us protect our well-being and foster a more authentic sense of self.About the SpeakerDr. Poonam Yadav is a psychologist and Assistant Professor at Gargi College, University of Delhi. With an M.Phil. and PhD from Jawaharlal Nehru University, her expertise spans social psychology, media psychology, and organizational behavior.Dr. Yadav has taught extensively on research methodology, personality development, and health and well-being. She is a published researcher who has presented her work at both national and international levels, bringing a deep academic rigor to the study of how modern media shapes the human mind. Beyond her academic role, she holds a Diploma in Guidance and Counselling, bridging the gap between theoretical research and applied psychological support.All Grounds for Thought tickets include a ₹250 coupon redeemable on food and beverages at the venue.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

description: The Procrastireaders: The May Escape (Bring Your Own Book)OverviewEscape the May heat and find refuge in a dedicated getaway for readers. The only goal for this session is to finally make progress on that book sitting on your nightstand. "The May Escape" is designed for those who want to reclaim their reading time in a quiet and comfortable setting.What is Stories of Hope?We are a community led initiative dedicated to creating safe and mindful spaces. Through intimate workshops and gatherings, we help individuals navigate life’s complexities from mental well being to personal growth with empathy and shared human connection.What to ExpectDeep Work for Reading: We will have a longer and silent reading block to help everyone actually get into a flow state.The "Currently Reading" Wall: A quick and casual way to share what everyone is reading and swap recommendations with the group.Slow Morning: No themes or prompts this time. Just a quiet and air conditioned space to read in peace and enjoy the company of fellow bookworms.Bring Your Own Book: Carry whatever you are currently reading or something from your "to be read" pile that needs your attention.Details🗓️ Date: 30th May (Saturday)⏰ Time: 11:30 AM📍 Location: Unlocked, 32nd Avenue🎟️ Fee: ₹700

description: A Table & A Print is a small evening at the farm built around making, eating, and spending time a little more slowly.The experience begins with a guided two-hour linocut printmaking session. Guests will be introduced to the process, create a design, carve it, and print it onto a cotton tote bag and paper to take home.After the workshop, we step outside for cocktails while the table is set, before returning inside for a six-course supper served one course at a time.This is an open invitation. Come with someone you love, or come on your own.MenuTo begin, outside Garden Breeze cucumber, basil, gin, lime, honey, sodaAt the tableThe PT Hummus with grilled zucchini, red peppers, and king trumpet mushroom non-vegetarian version with chicken skewersThe Collective Greens green beans, peas, edamame, cucumber, red onion, salad greens, toasted nuts and seeds, with plum mustard vinaigrette on a bed of labnehMidsummer Sunset Aperol, gin, citrus, berryMarcella’s Linguine with tomato butter sauce and ricotta or burrataThe Farm Roast butter-infused roast chicken with creamy truffle mash. Vegetarian alternative: roasted mushrooms with truffle mashBerry Gelato a bright, house-made berry gelatoAffogato vanilla ice cream finished with hot espressoDate: Saturday, 9 May 2026Time: 5:30pm onwardsVenue: IDIS Collective, DelhiPrice: ₹4,500 per personLimited seatsNote:No prior lino cut experience is needed.Vegetarian alternative available for the main course.Booking is essential.

description: Chor Bizarre hosts a Mahfil-e-Dastangoi, the 13th-century art of storytelling, once heard in royal courts and public gatherings across the subcontinent.Directed by Syed Sahil Agha, with performances by Sahil Sharma, Shadab Khan, and Diksha Tiwari, the evening brings alive tales of the Red Fort and Old Delhi - through voice, rhythm, and imagination alone.At Chor Bizarre, the table makes space for stories again! Come join us.

name: Street Art Tour at Lodhi Art District
description: We Invite you to take a walking tour in India’s first open sky art gallery. Lodhi Art District houses over 60 paintings on the walls of government residential buildings. Artists from Japan, New Zealand, Italy, Singapore, Argentina, Netherlands, Australia, USA, Canada, France, Mexico, Germany, Switzerland, Spain, Brazil, China, Nepal and India have painted the walls with unique themes highlighting variety of issues. The First Lady of France Briggitte Macron and Apple CEO Tim Cook had visited this art District on their visit to New Delhi. To cover this huge art District you would need definitely an art lover who explain each painting in detail. We Invite you to take most Instagrammable tour of Delhi with us. Note : We will cover 25 paintings.

name: The Story of Plastic
description: The Story of Plastic is a searing exposé revealing the ugly truth behind plastic pollution and the false solution of plastic recycling. Different from every other plastic documentary you’ve seen, The Story of Plastic presents a cohesive timeline of how we got to our current global plastic pollution crisis and how the oil and gas industry has successfully manipulated the narrative around it. From the extraction of fossil fuels and plastic disposal to the global resistance fighting back, The Story of Plastic is a life-changing, Emmy-winning film depicting one of the world’s most pressing environmental issues.

name: The Support Club
description: The Support ClubA No Taboo x Scoop Studio experienceWhat if the problem isn’t your body… but the bra you’ve been told fits it?Welcome to The Support Club! An intimate, small-group experience where we unpack everything you thought you knew about bras, sizing, and support… and replace it with something that actually works for your body.Co-hosted by Neelakshi (@plumptopretty and No Taboo Experiences) and Rhea (Bra Fitter and Founder of @shop_scoop_studio), this 90-minute session blends honest conversations, interactive exercises, and a personalized fitting to help you finally understand what real support feels like.Expect:A safe, judgment-free space to talk about bodies, bras, and everything in betweenA guided “bra reality check” to understand what’s actually going on with your current fitSimple, practical tools to decode sizing, support, and comfortA 1:1 personalized fitting by RheaAMA with Neelakshi (confidence to lingerie tips and tricks)A chance to try and shop a curated edit of fuller-bust friendly brasSo, whether you’ve always struggled with fit, constantly adjust your straps, or know something isn’t quite right: this is for you. You don’t leave with just a bra.You leave knowing your body.A quick note on sizingMost people who come to Scoop Studio are wearing the wrong size for their bodies.We’ve seen 36DD become 32G, 38E become 36H, and 34DD become 28H. So many people go from constantly adjusting to finally feeling supported for the first time.This session is designed to help you understand your body and find what truly works for you, not just what you’ve been told your size is.Available sizes: 28-44 Bands | DD-K Cups

description: 🍷 Wine lovers, this one’s for you.We’re hosting a guided wine tasting & pairing experience as summer approaches. 5 different seasonal wines, each paired with thoughtfully curated small bites, led by a sommelier who genuinely lives and breathes wine.This isn’t just “drink and eat.” It’s a wine journey — learning how flavours change, why pairings work, and how to enjoy wine more intuitively.✨ What makes it one-of-a-kind: • A guided tasting, not a lecture • 5 wines × 5 curated small bites with each tasting  and a grazing table to end the night.  A cosy, social table where you’ll meet new people who love food & wineIt’s relaxed, friendly, and absolutely young-wine-lover approved — come uncork with us 🍇

name: Chandini Chowk – Spiritual and Living Heritage
description: Chandni Chowk is not just a marketplace; it is a living museum where the 17th-century Mughal urban design breathes alongside the chaos of modern commerce.Designed in 1650 by Jahanara Begum, emperor Shah Jahan’s daughter, Chandni Chowk was once a grand canal-side boulevard. The water from Faiz Nahar flowed through its centre, reflecting the moonlight and giving the street its name. Today, the canal is gone, but the flow remains in the form of a relentless human tide.We will wander the interfaith mile, a single stretch of road where major places of worship for different faiths have coexisted for centuries.Join Mera शहर — My City Walks for a morning of storytelling as we navigate our way through the Kutcha’s and Katra’s of Chandini Chowk. This isn't just a walk through a marketplace; it’s a journey through a 375-year-old living museum where every turn of a lane reveals a layer of Delhi’s complex identity.

location.address: Chandni Chowk, Delhi, India

name: Caught in the Middle | Online
description: Often called the “sandwich generation,” this phase of life sits between responsibility and resilience.If you’re in your 40s or 50s, you probably don’t need an explanation, you are living it. Caring for aging parents while still showing up fully for your children. Holding space for everyone else, while trying to keep your own world from unraveling in the process.It is a lot. And it’s often invisible.This is a space to pause, share, and connect with others who understand the weight of it, without needing to explain it. Come talk to people who get it.

location.address: Delhi, India

name: Beyond The Eye: A Talk On Hidden Self-Harm
description: Join us for a lecture that looks at a question we rarely ask honestly:What if self-harm isn’t always visible, and not as rare as we think?About the LectureSelf-harm is often spoken about in extremes: visible, alarming, and distant from everyday life.But what if that picture is incomplete?This lecture shifts the conversation from the obvious to the subtle, asking how self-detrimental behaviours show up in ways we don’t always recognise. It challenges the assumption that self-harm is something “other people” do, and instead examines how many of us may engage in forms of it, consciously or not.The session explores:How self-harm is defined — beyond its most visible formsWhy it remains heavily stigmatised and misunderstoodWhat drives people to act against their own well-beingHow seemingly ordinary behaviours can take on self-destructive patternsAt its core, this talk asks a more unsettling question: Why do humans sometimes act against their own survival? and what does that reveal about how we cope, adapt, and endure?About the SpeakerSasha Bansal is a PhD scholar at Ashoka University, with an MSc in Psychological Sciences from the State University of New York.Her research focuses on self-harming behaviour, psychometrics, social psychology, the psychology of religion, and advanced statistical methods. With over ten academic publications, she has presented her work at international platforms including The NorthEastern Evolutionary Psychology Society Conference.She is also the founder of StatPsyched, an initiative aimed at making research and statistics education more accessible to students worldwide.All Grounds for Thought tickets include a ₹250 coupon redeemable on food and beverages at the venue. About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

name: Sham-e-Qutub : An evening heritage walk
description: We are delighted to invite you to a special *evening heritage walk at Qutub Minar*, curated by Darwesh Heritage Walks**Date:2nd May**Time:6:00 p.m. onwardsExperience the magic of one of Delhi’s most iconic monuments as it transforms at dusk—when the Qutub Minar complex is beautifully lit and wrapped in an almost timeless charm.This walk takes you through the story of Delhi’s first capital at Mehrauli, tracing the rise of the Tomars and Chauhans, and the arrival of the Delhi Sultanate under the early Turko-Afghan rulers. As the evening sets in, the illuminated ruins, soaring minar, and intricate carvings create a truly immersive atmosphere—perfect for experiencing history not just as a narrative, but as a living, breathing space.During this walk, we will explore:* The origins of Delhi’s earliest city at Mehrauli* The political transitions from Rajput to Sultanate rule* The architectural brilliance of the Qutub complex and its symbolism* The stories hidden in its mosques, pillars, and inscriptionsJoin us for an unforgettable evening of storytelling, history, and exploration—where the past comes alive under the soft glow of lights.We look forward to having you with us.(Please note: the fee is not for access to the site, but rather for curation of this experience)

location.address: Seth Sarai, Mehrauli, New Delhi, Delhi 110030, India

name: Safdarjung Tomb : A Heritage Walk
description: We are delighted to invite you to a unique and immersive heritage walk experience at Safdarjung Tomb, curated by Darwesh Heritage walks in collaboration with Sabhyata Foundation.Date: 3rd MayTime: 8:30 a.m.Step into a fascinating chapter of Delhi’s layered past as we explore Safdarjung Tomb—often described as the *“last flicker in the lamp of Mughal architecture.”*Built in 1754 for Nawab Safdarjung, the powerful Nawab of Awadh and Prime Minister of the Mughal Empire, the monument stands at a time when imperial grandeur was gradually fading. Through its imposing structure and classic charbagh garden layout, the tomb reflects both the elegance of Mughal design and the subtle signs of a declining empire.During this walk, we will explore:• The life and legacy of Safdarjung and the rise of Awadh• The shifting political landscape of late Mughal Delhi• Architectural transitions that mark the end of an eraJoin us for a morning of engaging storytelling, history, and immersive exploration at one of Delhi’s most evocative yet often overlooked monuments.We look forward to having you with us.Please note: the fee is not for access to the site, but rather for curation of this experience.

location.address: Airforce Golf Course, Delhi Race Club, New Delhi, Delhi 110003, India

name: The Poet at the Brunch Table 2.0
description: “I’d argue that poets, when looking at a menu, first fall in love with the sound of the dish; then the food comes out and we fall in love with the sight of the dish. And when we finally eat, we fall in love with the taste. This sequence is what I call the sensory tease,” says poet Dorothy Chan.To turn the sensory tease into an experience, Dillipoetry and The Fio Table invite you to The Poet at the Brunch Table 2.0—a two-hour immersive workshop that brings into play food as a genre melded with reflective writing exercises to help one delve into themes of intimacy, memories, loss & friendships. Here, brunch is a metaphor, and plate is a page.With Dillipoetry’s signature coalescing approach to verse and The Fio Table’s ode to the Mediterranean coastline, the workshop will encompass self-probing, active participation, creative writing exercises, and facilitated reflection. We will also discuss rituals & traditions across cultures that brought together food & poetry - and what mark did this interaction leave in the world of literature.Bring you friends, and leave your writing blocks (and diet) at the door. We can only accommodate a maximum of 12 people for now. Registration will close once we reach capacity. About the host: Rachit is a poet and founder of Dillipoetry—a literary platform known for its thematic, experimental poetry walks, workshops, and training programmes. With over a decade in the social development sector, his work spans diverse social issues. His poetry has appeared in 25+ literary platforms and anthologies. His recent series Memories of Yami and The Asthma City—tracing the Yamuna River through Delhi and the city’s escalating air crisis—were published by Down To Earth magazine.Rachit has been selected for international residencies, including Language is a Queer Thing, a programme by The Queer Muslim Project in collaboration with the British Council, UK. He received the Germinator Grant by Ecoversity for Dillipoetry’s regenerative approach to arts and community.He has performed twice at BBC Contains Strong Language—the UK’s largest poetry festival (Leeds 2023, Bradford 2025)—and at institutions including the French Institute India, Goethe-Institut, British Council, and Alliance Française. His work has appeared on BBC Radio 3’s The Verb. He also served as Lead Poet and Advisor to O.P. Jindal Global University’s programme, We Are Meant to Sing as One.

name: Improv Comedy Show: Play2Perform Level 2
description: After 14 hours of high-energy, no-holds-barred workshopping, this ensemble—now working at Level 2—steps into performance with sharper instincts, bolder choices, and a deepened sense of play. What unfolds is completely unscripted, wildly unpredictable, and built in the momenThis isn’t just a show you watch. It’s one you fuel.Your suggestions spark scenes. Your ideas twist the story. Your laughter drives the rhythm.A single word can spiral into a love story, a disaster, or something beautifully absurd. One performer holds the thread, the rest run with it—and somehow, chaos turns into magic.No scripts. No second takes. Just quick thinking, deep listening, and fearless fun.Expect moments that land perfectly, moments that go hilariously sideways, and everything in between. That’s where the real joy lives.HighlightsFully improvised, unscripted comedyAudience participation shapes the showPerformed by Level 2 trained ensembleHigh-energy, interactive experienceEvery show is completely uniqueAbout the HostAnshu DagaAn improv facilitator and performer guiding immersive, high-energy workshops that focus on spontaneity, presence, and creative expression.Come for the spontaneity. Stay for the surprises.Don’t plan the night—just secure your seat!

name: Journey Of Hearts - Delhi Open Mic
description: Not every love story is romantic.Some are built in kitchens, in quiet sacrifices, in sleepless nights,in words never spoken but always felt.This Mother’s Day, Journey of Hearts invites you to bring your truth to the stage.A poem for your mother.A letter you never sent.A story of distance, healing, or becoming.This isn’t about perfection.It’s about honesty.About the kind of love that shapes us—even when we don’t understand it.So if your heart carries something unfinished,this is your space to let it breathe.✨ Journey of Hearts – DelhiA Mother’s Day special open mic by Likhon Dil Se.

name: LOUDR presents MAKE SENSE at goSTOPS Plus Gurugram
description: Make Sense is a 60-minute interactive workshop where you’ll create stories using all five senses - what you see, hear, touch, smell and feel. Through a series of simple, guided prompts, you’ll work with sensory cues to build a short narrative from scratch, turning everyday perceptions into something imaginative and unexpected.Working in small groups, you’ll shape your ideas into a performative moment and bring your story to life. The session is designed to be playful, collaborative, and open to everyone. No prior experience in theatre or storytelling is needed. Just show up, participate, and see what you can make sense of.

description: The Table Wears PradaSunday, 3rd May | 12:30 PM – 3:30 PMStep into a table where fashion meets flavour, and every detail feels straight out of The Devil Wears Prada.This is not just a brunch, it’s a curated afternoon of vintage luxury in collabration with KURO , sharp aesthetics, and storytelling through food. name: From Plate To Planet
description: Join us for a session that dismantles the idea of health as not a purely individual choice but also reveals it as a biological and communal dialogue shaped by the trillions of microbes living within us.About the LectureHealth is often treated as a matter of personal willpower: something determined solely by the gym or a specific diet. This lecture begins from the opposite premise.Drawing on years of research in metagenomics and microbiome science, this session examines the human body as a complex, plural ecosystem. Focusing on the fascinating "gut-brain axis," the lecture traces how microbial communities behave, communicate, and influence everything from our immune system to our emotional resilience.Moving across themes of dietary shifts, the rise of ultra-processed foods, and approaches to wellbeing, the lecture explores:The Gut-Brain Highway: How the communication network between microbes and the brain shapes our mental landscape.The Standard American Diet (SAD): The impact of modern, low-fiber eating patterns on microbial diversity and rising levels of anxiety.The Inner Ecosystem: Understanding the trillions of microbes that play a silent role in our daily survival.Ubuntu and Interconnectedness: How health grows from the relationship between people, food systems, and the environmentEveryday Stewardship: How small, thoughtful actions can care for both our internal world and the planet.We are "holobionts"—communities of life that require kindness, awareness, and purpose to thrive. It asks how we can reclaim our health by understanding the invisible connections that bind us to our food, our communities, and our world.Flow of the Evening4:00 PM – Doors open: Come in, find your seat, and get comfortable.4:05 – 4:20 PM – Place your orders and start a few conversations.4:30 PM – The learning begins.5:15 PM – Open Q&A and discussion with Dr. Brahma.5:30 PM onwards – Stay a while: More eating, more drinking, more conversations.About the SpeakerDr. Vijaya Brahma is a microbiologist and the Director of Ubuntuverse, a community dedicated to living with purpose and kindness. Her scientific journey began with studying microbes during her PhD at CSIR IMTECH and continued through postdoctoral research at the University of Toronto.Today, she works at the intersection of science and everyday life through the Ubuntu Wellness Academy. She is the host of the YouTube channel Shodh Ka Pratibodh, where she humanizes the scientific journey by interviewing researchers about their paths beyond just academic achievements. Much of her work involves mentoring and science communication, turning complex metagenomic research into simple, actionable conversations about the gut microbiome and planetary health.About unLectureunLecture brings experts out of classrooms and into cafés, bars and neighbourhood spaces across Delhi. Created by three friends from college who wanted learning to feel warm, social and alive. unLecture turns an otherwise ordinary evening into a conversation you may think about long after it ends.

description: Step into a space where spontaneity meets performance.Improv Level One – From Play to Performance is a 14-hour immersive improv theatre workshop at The Immersion Room, Gurgaon, designed for adults who want to explore creativity, confidence, and live expression.Through a series of interactive theatre games, scene work, and character exploration, participants develop:Spontaneity & quick thinkingPresence & active listeningConfidence in expressionCollaboration & trustStorytelling through performanceThis workshop focuses on learning by doing—breaking inhibitions, embracing uncertainty, and discovering the joy of being fully present.Multi-day Weekend Workshop SeriesMay 3, 2026 | 11:00 AM – 1:00 PMMay 10, 2026 | 11:00 AM – 1:00 PMMay 17, 2026 | 11:00 AM – 1:00 PMMay 24, 2026 | 12:00 PM – 2:00 PMMay 31, 2026 | 11:00 AM – 1:00 PMJune 7, 2026 | 11:00 AM – 1:00 PMJune 14, 2026 | 11:00 AM – 1:00 PMTotal Duration: 14 HoursFormat: In-person | Adults OnlyFinal Showcase:The experience culminates in a live improv performance at The Immersion Room, where participants step onto the stage and perform in front of an audience.

name: Mere Humnafas
description: Mere Humnafas is an intimate, immersive musical–storytelling experience that reconnects contemporary audiences with the emotional and cultural roots of the subcontinent’s rich poetic and musical traditions. At its core lies a simple yet powerful idea: to create something truly new, we must first engage deeply with the masters who shaped our artistic language.For generations, we’ve been reminded that innovation doesn’t exist in isolation—it grows through dialogue with history, memory, and those who came before us. Mere Humnafas brings this philosophy to life by revisiting timeless ghazals, nazms, and poetic expressions that have shaped our collective emotional landscape. These classics are reimagined through contemporary acoustic arrangements, featuring only a keyboard and acoustic guitar, allowing the poetry and emotion to take center stage with striking intimacy.Woven through the music is a narrative that explores enduring themes of love, longing, separation, lost affection, and unspoken devotion—emotions that remain deeply relevant today. Through spoken word and reflection, the experience bridges generations, offering a modern lens while honoring the depth and beauty of the original voices.Mere Humnafas invites you into a dreamy, almost surreal evening—an hour where past and present dissolve into one, where memory and imagination intertwine, and where music and poetry remind us of the power of feeling deeply.This is not just a performance.It is a return—to our roots, our stories, and to each other.

name: Guzaarish
description: Kathika in collaboration with Vrindan invites you all to experience Guzaarish, an evocative musical experience that weaves together the soulful strands of thumri, ghazal, raga and rhythm into a richly layered soundscape. Inspired by the intimate acoustics of traditional havelis, where music once echoed through courtyards and corridors, this concert brings alive a timeless era of expression and artistry.Presented by Vrindan and performed by internationally acclaimed artists, Guzaarish is not just a concert, but a living, breathing exchange between performers and audience. Through a unique request-led format, the evening unfolds organically, allowing listeners to shape the journey and reconnect with melodies that hold personal meaning.Rooted in the golden era of semi-classical music and infused with the charm of vintage cinema, each rendition carries nostalgia, poetic depth and emotional resonance. From beloved classics to hidden gems, Guzaarish invites you to rediscover, reminisce, and revel in a shared musical journey, one that is as intimate as it is unforgettable.

name: Open SketchBook
description: Open SketchBook: You don’t need to leave the city to feel grounded.OverviewOur core belief is that you do not need to leave the city to feel grounded. In a world where everyone is distracted, we pause as a group to give our attention to one shared thing. Sketching becomes the medium to notice small, living details in urban spaces that we usually rush through.You do not need to be an artist to enjoy sketching. Open SketchBook is a session for anyone who wants to explore drawing as a way to relax and observe the world. It is about the process of putting pencil to paper rather than the final masterpiece.What is Stories of Hope?We are a community led initiative dedicated to creating safe and mindful spaces. Through intimate workshops and gatherings, we help individuals navigate life’s complexities from mental well being to personal growth with empathy and shared human connection.What to expectGuided Observation: Learn simple ways to look at objects or spaces and translate them into lines and shapes.Creative Freedom: A judgment free zone where you can experiment with sketching without worrying about "doing it right."Tactile Experience: Use your new diary and pencil kit to capture what you see in the moment.Relaxed Environment: Enjoy a late afternoon at Greenr with a small group of people who just want to create and connect.Details🗓️ Date: 19th April (Sunday)⏰ Time: 5:00 PM📍 Location: Greenr, Golf Course Ext Road🎟️ Contribution: ₹700 (Includes an A6 diary and pencil kit)

The Listening Room is a unique session dedicated to the art of being present. It is a space to share, to hear others, and to practice the kind of attention that builds real connection and empathy.What is Stories of Hope?We are a community led initiative dedicated to creating safe and mindful spaces. Through intimate workshops and gatherings, we help individuals navigate life’s complexities from mental well being to personal growth with empathy and shared human connection.What to expectActive Listening: Practice being fully present while others speak without the need to interrupt or give immediate advice.Shared Stories: A safe environment to share your own thoughts or experiences and feel truly heard by a small group.Quiet Reflection: Moments of silence to process what has been shared and to notice how deep listening changes our perspective.Supportive Connection: A morning at Greenr focused on human presence and the simple power of witnessing each other’s stories.Details🗓️ Date: 19th April (Sunday)⏰ Time: 11:00 AM📍 Location: Greenr, Golf Course Ext Road🎟️ Contribution: ₹700

It is a powerful look at what it means to keep going when everything seems to be falling apart.What is Stories of Hope?We are a community led initiative dedicated to creating safe and mindful spaces. name: The Drafting Table
description: The Drafting Table: The Devil Wears Prada - ReimaginedOverviewStep into the world of editorial high stakes and creative reinvention. This session invites you to take the foundation of a classic story and make it entirely your own. Whether you want to keep the iconic setting or flip the script completely, "The Drafting Table" provides the structure and space to transform familiar characters and choices into a fresh narrative.What is Stories of Hope?We are a community led initiative dedicated to creating safe and mindful spaces. Through intimate workshops and gatherings, we help individuals navigate life’s complexities from mental well being to personal growth with empathy and shared human connection.What to ExpectCreative Freedom: Reimagine the core of "The Devil Wears Prada." name: Overthinkers Anonymous
description: OverviewEver wondered why you default to people pleasing, perfectionism, or avoidance? Overthinkers Anonymous is a small and guided space to slow down, reflect, and talk honestly about the things that occupy our minds. This session focuses on Family Patterns and how the environments we grew up in still shape how we think, feel, and respond today.We focus on connecting the dots between past experiences and present reactions in a safe and judgment free environment. The goal is to build awareness and self compassion rather than focusing on blame.What is Stories of Hope?We are a community-led initiative dedicated to creating safe, mindful spaces. Through workshops and gatherings, we help individuals navigate life’s complexities, from mental well-being to personal growth, with empathy and shared human connection.What to expectPattern Recognition: Explore why tendencies like overthinking or control keep showing up in your life.Guided Reflection: Use a specialized worksheet to gain context on your reactions without the weight of self-judgment.Awareness & Compassion: A gentle space to build self-awareness and understanding—focused on insight, not on reliving trauma.Details🗓️ Date: 12th April (Sunday)⏰ Time: 11:30 AM📍 Location: Thamel, Golf Course Ext Road🎟️ Contribution: ₹700 (Includes Coffee + Snacks)

name: The Procrastireaders
description: OverviewStep out of your literary comfort zone and into a world of mystery. Our "Blind Date with a Book" session is designed to strip away the influence of flashy covers and famous names. It lets you connect with a story based purely on its essence. This is a low stakes and high reward way to discover your next favorite read while sharing a brew with fellow book lovers.What is Stories of Hope?We are a community led initiative dedicated to creating safe and mindful spaces. Through intimate workshops and gatherings, we help individuals navigate life’s complexities from mental well being to personal growth with empathy and shared human connection.What to ExpectThe Concept: Browse a curated selection of books from the SOH library wrapped in brown paper. You will not see titles or authors. Only a few plot keywords or a "vibe" will guide your choice.The Surprise: Follow your intuition. Pick the one that intrigues you most based solely on the cryptic clues written on the front.First Impressions: We will spend the first part of the session "meeting" our books, unmasking the titles, and seeing if you have found a literary match.Low Stakes: This is a fun and pressure free way to break out of your usual genre. You might try something you would never have picked up otherwise.Details🗓️ Date: 16th May (Saturday)⏰ Time: 5:30 PM📍 Location: Thamel, DLF Phase 1☕ Inclusion: Coffee included🎟️ Fee: ₹700

name: Before You Blame
description: Before You Blame: A Workshop on Navigating Relationships with ClarityOverviewMost relationship conflicts are not just about what happened; they are about how we interpret what happened. This session focuses on exploring how our thoughts shape our connections and why friction often feels more intense than the situation warrants. "Before You Blame" is a reflective workshop designed to help you look at your assumptions and inner patterns, moving you from reactivity toward steadiness and emotional responsibility.What is Stories of Hope?We are a community-led initiative dedicated to creating safe and mindful spaces. Through intimate workshops and gatherings, we help individuals navigate life’s complexities, from mental well-being to personal growth, with empathy and shared human connection.What to ExpectPattern Recognition: Explore how common emotional triggers and ego-driven patterns shape conflicts in marriage, family, or work.Internal Focus: Instead of trying to fix others, we gently shift the focus inward to understand our own interpretations and responses.Practical Tools: Learn actionable ways to question stressful thoughts and practice perspective shifts using real-life scenarios.Safe Exploration: A supportive, non-judgmental environment for honest discussion. This is a space for learning and reflection, not a lecture or therapy.Key TakeawaysClarity: Gain a deeper understanding of recurring relationship dynamics.Responsiveness: Walk away with tools to help you respond thoughtfully instead of reacting impulsively.Emotional Ownership: Build the internal steadiness needed to navigate life without requiring others to change first.Details🗓️ Date: 16th May (Sunday)⏰ Time: 11:00 AM📍 Location: Kanchanjunga Towers, Sector 56🎟️ Fee: ₹900 (Introductory offer: ₹200 off original price of ₹1100)

name: ALT EFF Film Club x GuruJal Gurugram | 15th May
description: Join us for a punchy lineup of 4 short films (2 Indian and 2 international), each bringing its own flavour of environmental storytelling.And then… stay for ALT EFF Voices, our signature open mic where audience members share short, personal, environment-related stories. No practice needed. Just pick a prompt from a hat and dive in. SHORT FILM PROGRAMME:A brilliant selection of cinematic, poetic, diverse pieces of environmental storytelling that are short enough to get you hooked and never long enough to bore! Some Thoughts on the Common ToadThe FeastThe Butterfly ManThe Floating worldTo become a Film Club Member, continue with the regular booking process. The membership option will appear after you select the venue and date.All activities, including discussions and refreshments, will occur between 7:00 and 9:00 PM.What's Included?Film screening + Open MicGroup discussionLight refreshmentsStudent Discount50% off for students with a valid ID (& must bring ID to the screening).CODE: ALTSTUDENTABOUT ALT EFFIf you are tired of the outrage cycle, doomscrolling collapse, and the looming AI takeover narrative. If you have caught yourself going numb just to cope. We see you.All Living Things Environmental Film Festival is back with the third season of Film Club. A monthly meet up across 12 cities over 5 months, orbiting environmental cinema that cracks you open without trapping you in doom and gloom.It is a room full of people choosing to show up in cities whose infrastructure persuades you to stay home, choosing to sit together, watch together, and turn private anxiety into collective imagination. Messy. Human. Honest conversation. With snacks. Always snacks.
